intent:
Mother and I climb to the apartment roof at evening; one cloud sits high in the sky and I watch it. I tell mother I want to keep watching, and she answers — playfully, in her bright voice — that the cloud is shaped like bread. I quietly disagree: it isn't bread. She watches it with me anyway. The change-of-state is from solitary watching (s2) to a shared looking that tolerates a small disagreement (s6). The anchor (cloud) sits high and is the thing both characters keep returning to.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 10)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator REVEALS, by quiet contradiction, that mother's playful naming of the cloud ("it's bread") does not match what the narrator sees, and mother joins the watching anyway.
  change_of_state:       at s0, narrator and mother step out (standing) onto the roof and the cloud has not been named; at closer, the cloud has been named (by mother), un-named (by narrator), and the two have settled (sitting) onto the roof together — solitary watching → shared, contradicted, settled-together.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a phone_call / rainy_doorway / shop_counter story. Scene_class is rooftop (first time in corpus), per seed_plan slot 10.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 雲 (cloud). Last appeared in story: never.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: physical-posture-change with mother as subject (mother sits down on the chair on the roof, with narrator). NOT a noun-pile, NOT a 「Nは Adj です」 mirror, NOT a P:でも-/P:だから- pattern, NOT a mirror of s2's watch-the-cloud-slowly. Sitting on a chair on the roof (not bare roof tile) — natural; reinforces 椅子 (debt) and bookends 屋根 from s0.
  why_not_filler:        Mother — necessary as the second voice and as the active sitter at the closer; without her there is no naming, no contradiction, no settled-together moment. Cloud — central; named, denied, watched. Roof — bookend object: stepped onto in s0, sat upon (via the rooftop chair) in s6. Sky — single descriptive frame for the cloud (one sentence). 夕方 — explains why this is a contemplative slot, not a busy one. ゆっくり — paces narrator's looking (s2) AND mother's settling (s6); both occurrences are causal (slow looking is the action; slow sitting is mother accepting the moment after the small disagreement).
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce N5_na_adj absorbed by mother's 「元気な声で…」 in s4 (organic — she is being playful); must-reinforce N5_demo absorbed by s5 「でも、その雲はパンではありません。」 (organic — narrator gently disagrees with mother's joke); new grammar N5_tai (〜たい polite want-to-do) absorbed by s3 「その雲を見たいです。」 (organic — child voices wanting-to-keep-watching to the parent, the textbook-canonical first appearance of the polite want-form, which causally triggers mother's reply at s4). NOT bolted on.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): phone_call, rainy_doorway, shop_counter
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): paper, towel, umbrella, 本, 電話
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): P:だから-noun-noun-WO-noun-X:います, P:でも-noun-WA-noun-NI-noun, noun-noun-WO-noun-GA-noun-WA-noun-X:います
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens):
    - 降る / 雨 / 冷たい
    - 私 / 本屋 / います
    - 今日 / 友達 / 学校

sentences.jp: 夕方、母と私は屋根に出ます。
sentences.en: In the evening, mother and I step out onto the roof.
In the sky there is a cloud.

sentences.jp: 私はその雲をゆっくり見ます。
私は「その雲を見たいです。」と母に話します。
sentences.en: I say to mother, "I want to watch that cloud."

sentences.jp: 母は元気な声で「その雲はパンですよ。」と答えます。
sentences.en: In a bright voice mother answers, "That cloud is bread."

sentences.jp: でも、その雲はパンではありません。
sentences.en: But that cloud is not bread.

sentences.jp: 母と私は一緒に屋根の椅子に座ります。
sentences.en: Mother and I sit down together on the chair on the roof.
